title: Fix crash on app startup before authentication screen

requirements.text: Fix the unhandled error that causes the app to crash and display 'Une erreur s'est produite / Erreur inconnue' before the authentication/login screen is even shown. The error is triggered during the app initialization phase (before Internet Identity loads), caught by the ErrorBoundary component, and renders the error card instead of the login page. Identify and fix the root cause in the initialization sequence (App.tsx, main.tsx, useInternetIdentity, or related bootstrap code) so the app reliably reaches the login screen on first load.

quotes: Voila lerreur avant meme de midentifier

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app at the root URL no longer shows the error card 'Une erreur s'est produite'
The login/authentication screen (LoginPage) is displayed correctly on fresh load without any prior interaction
No unhandled exceptions are thrown during the initialization phase before authentication
The ErrorBoundary does not trigger on normal app startup
The fix works on mobile Chrome (Android) as shown in the screenshots

requirements.text: Add defensive error handling in the app bootstrap and initialization code so that if an unexpected error does occur during startup, it is caught gracefully and the user is shown the login page or a recoverable state rather than a generic crash screen. Ensure all async initialization errors (e.g. from AuthClient, IndexedDB, service worker registration) are handled with try/catch blocks and do not propagate to the React error boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Any error during bootstrap (AuthClient init, IndexedDB setup, service worker registration) is caught and logged without crashing the UI
The app falls back gracefully to the login screen even if a non-critical initialization step fails
The ErrorBoundary is not triggered by initialization-phase errors

constraints: Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/main.tsx directly — propose safe alternatives or wrappers
Do not change the backend schema or Motoko code
nonGoals: Changing the visual design or theme of the app
Modifying authentication logic beyond fixing the crash
